body.title: [triage] Freeze/dedup retired TheIgors* build-artifact copies (archive tidiness)
body.description:
**⛔ FAIL — KICKED BACK TO DESIGN 2026-07-13 (Akien: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing').**

**THIS TICKET ENTERED SPRINT WITH NO INTENTION.** Under D-intentions-are-the-root-2026-07-13 and proof-on-close, THE INTENTION IS THE PROPERTY MUTATION-RED BREAKS. With no intention there is **no property to break**, so this ticket **cannot be proven as it stands** — it could only ever close hollow, or `shipped-unproven` for a reason that is not the real one.

**IT IS NOT DEFECTIVE WORK — IT IS INCOMPLETE DESIGN.** So the honest disposition is BACK TO DESIGN, not a backfilled intention. CC drafting the missing intention would have **papered over the real defect**: 150 tickets cleared the design step without an intention, which is a **gate hole**, not 150 individual oversights. Per CLAUDE.md, the nuisance stays VISIBLE so it drives finding the fix — never papered over, which sucks silently forever (CP4).

**CP2 — FAIL = FURTHER ADVANCE IN LEARNING.** This kickback IS the learning signal. The fix is upstream: the triage→sprint transition must reject a missing, empty, or degenerate (`TBD`/`unknown`) intention (T-sprint-tickets-with-no-intention-cannot-be-proven).

**TO RE-ENTER SPRINT:** state the intention as a present-tense contract — *'I intend that X, because Y'* — where X names a property a hollow build would violate. Akien declares it (`provenance_class: declared`); a machine-extracted one is a RATCHET, not a SPEC (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13).

---

I intend that the retired pre-UU build-artifact stashes are clearly frozen read-only archives with no redundant binary duplicates, so they cannot be mistaken for a live source of truth.

A filesystem sweep found build-artifact debt OUTSIDE the UU repo: (1) TheIgorsProject/design_docs/ and TheIgorsProject/lab/design_docs/ are binary-identical copies of each other (same mod times, decisions/ 117 vs 115 — a merge artifact); (2) TheIgorsProject still carries live (writable) design_docs/ and design_docs_for_igor/ + .dsb trees that CLAUDE.md marks retired; these should be frozen archive only. UU proper is clean (no .dsb, JSON-only) — the debt is entirely in the ~/TheIgors* trees.

**Affected files:** none in the UU repo — operates on ~/TheIgorsProject, ~/TheIgors.archive, ~/TheIgors-cert-walks (external archive dirs). **Design rules:** look at the target before deleting/overwriting (these are historical archives — do NOT blind-delete; verify each is a true duplicate and nothing live references it first). **Scope boundary:** IN — confirm the design_docs/ vs lab/design_docs/ copies are byte-identical, collapse to one, mark the TheIgors* archive trees read-only/clearly-frozen. OUT — touching anything under the UU repo; deleting any unique (non-duplicate) decision/ticket file (the full corpus is the substantiation for the book's '500+ decisions' claim — preserve all distinct records). **Test plan:** no automated test (out-of-repo archive housekeeping) because: this is one-time filesystem tidiness on frozen archives; verification is a manual diff + a re-run of the discovery sweep showing the duplicate pair collapsed.
**Completion criteria:** the design_docs/ vs lab/design_docs/ binary duplicate pair is collapsed to one copy; ~/TheIgors* artifact trees are read-only; a re-run of the artifact sweep no longer reports the duplication anomaly. No distinct decision/ticket record is lost.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.fail_reason: entered sprint with no intention — cannot be proven; design incomplete

title: Merge distinct records, then DELETE superseded TheIgors* artifact copies
description:
I intend that the retired pre-UU build-artifact stashes hold only DISTINCT records that haven't been migrated, and that every record already merged into the canonical store has its old copy DELETED — so the corpus lives in exactly one home and we never stumble over scattered duplicates again.

A sweep found the same decision/ticket corpus spread across five locations (TheIgorsProject x2, TheIgors.archive, TheIgors-cert-walks, current UU) — ~1,300 raw decision files, mostly duplicates, because prior migrations copied-forward but never removed-behind. Akien's rule (2026-06-25): when artifacts are successfully merged/migrated into canonical, DELETE the old copies. See feedback_delete_after_successful_merge.

Order is verify-merged -> then delete, never blind delete. (1) Identify which archived decisions/tickets are DISTINCT and not yet in devlab/runtime/memory/. (2) Migrate those distinct records into canonical and VERIFY they landed. (3) Then delete the redundant/superseded copies across the TheIgors* trees (including the byte-identical design_docs/ vs lab/design_docs/ pair) so one home survives. No distinct record is lost; every confirmed duplicate is removed.

**Affected files:** none in the UU repo for the delete step — operates on ~/TheIgorsProject, ~/TheIgors.archive, ~/TheIgors-cert-walks; migration step writes into devlab/runtime/memory/ via memory_emit.py. **Design rules:** look at the target before deleting (confirm true duplicate / confirm merged first); one-home-no-exceptions; delete-after-successful-merge (feedback). **Scope boundary:** IN — diff archives vs canonical, migrate distinct records, verify, delete confirmed-duplicate/superseded copies, collapse the binary-identical design_docs pair. OUT — deleting any record that is distinct and NOT yet merged (migrate it first); touching the UU repo's own tracked files. **Test plan:** no automated test (out-of-repo archive housekeeping) because verification is: a diff showing every deleted file's content exists in canonical, plus a re-run of the artifact sweep reporting zero duplicate stashes.
**Completion criteria:** re-running the artifact discovery sweep reports a single canonical decision/ticket home with no duplicate archives; every record that was deleted is provably present in devlab/runtime/memory/; the design_docs/ vs lab/design_docs/ duplicate pair is gone.
